description: Treat package scripts as repository contracts and change them only with caller impact checked.
body: Use when adding or modifying npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun scripts. Do not rewrite script names to personal preference when CI, docs, or workspace tooling already calls them. Search for script usage, preserve package manager conventions, and prefer package-local commands. Verify the narrow script and any aggregate script that references it. Risk boundary is CI breakage from script name drift or lockfile mismatch. Use when JavaScript Runtime and Tooling work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
